video_title: Sui Co-Founder: No Funds Were Lost In The Outage - Here's Why
gist: Sui co-founder Costas (ex-Facebook Libra lead cryptographer) explains the recent multi-hour Sui outage as an edge case from a six-month address-balances feature, stressing that no funds were lost because the network halts by design rather than risk losing money. He pitches Sui's edge for the agentic economy: free stablecoin transfers, object-based parallel execution, zero-knowledge primitives, decentralized key management (Seal), and Walrus storage for AI agent memory.

insights.claim: The recent multi-hour Sui network outage stemmed from a new address-balances feature with a gas-metering bug on the validator side, an edge case from a missed net case during a six-month build.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Frames the failure as a contained engineering edge case rather than a systemic flaw, which matters for assessing SUI protocol risk after the incident.
insights.quote: There was an issue with the gas massing and this code uh like um an issue on the validator side uh which created some outads uh for a few hours.

insights.claim: No funds or balances were lost in the Sui outage because the network is designed to halt at the consensus layer rather than continue operating and risk losing money.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: A halt-over-loss safety posture is a key durability argument for holding or building on SUI despite downtime risk.
insights.quote: as you realize there was no money lost in this situation and this is by design

insights.claim: Fixing the initial Sui bug created a low-probability cascading situation that could trigger a similar issue before a permanent solution was found after a couple of iterations.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Signals the outage was not a clean single fix, relevant for gauging residual fragility in recently shipped Sui code.

insights.claim: Sui's incident response involved a war room of more than 30 to 50 people plus external auditors coordinating across time zones, with validators needing to be educated on what fix they were voting to restore.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Recovery speed depends on validator coordination and awake operators, a structural constraint investors should weight when assessing decentralized chain downtime.
insights.quote: it's not one company pressing a button right and you are up in the next moment You have to go to the validators

insights.claim: The Sui co-founder contrasts the recovery favorably against Facebook's old 'move fast and break things' motto, arguing another blockchain without Sui's team would not have recovered even in days.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Team quality is being positioned as the moat that justifies SUI's risk premium over rival chains.
insights.quote: if it was in another blockchain that issue without having these brains behind it, I don't believe they would recover even even in in in days

insights.claim: Sui now offers free stablecoin transfers, which the co-founder calls one of its biggest wins for onboarding non-crypto users who cannot conceptualize paying a fractional gas fee.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Free stablecoin rails position SUI as payments infrastructure competing with modern banking, a growth vector beyond trading.
insights.quote: in my opinion one of the best features that we are offering now is that payments for stable coins are for free

insights.claim: Sui lets users log in with Facebook or Google accounts without long passwords, lowering the first-time barrier to using blockchain for payments.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Web2-style social login is a UX wedge for mainstream adoption that could differentiate SUI's user funnel.

insights.claim: Free stablecoin transfers let regulated banks transact in stablecoins without holding native crypto, avoiding the accounting problem of paying gas in SUI when intending to move dollars.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Removing native-token gas friction opens an institutional and banking use case for SUI rails.

insights.claim: Sui's object-based model with parallel execution is positioned as the natural way for AI agents to understand ownership, contrasting with Ethereum and Solana which compressed away objects to optimize performance.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: If agents prefer object semantics, SUI could capture automated trading and RWA flows that rivals' account models handle less naturally.
insights.quote: the object is a natural way for anything AI to understand ownership

insights.claim: The co-founder argues a single fast L1 avoids the silo and liquidity-fragmentation problem of L2s, which fight each other, especially on Ethereum.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: A monolithic-L1 thesis is the core competitive bet against the Ethereum rollup-centric roadmap.
insights.quote: all of the liquidity is there it's not split between the L2s and everything

insights.claim: Agentic transactions require longer messages and multi-operation payment intents in one transaction, which the co-founder says slower L1s cannot handle, citing a single transaction that buys 1% of Burj Khalifa, sells for gold, then buys dollars.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Complex single-transaction composability is the use case SUI claims as its moat for agentic and RWA finance.

insights.claim: Sui pairs with Walrus, built by the same Mysten Labs team, to provide fast portable memory for AI agents to store heavy data, which the co-founder says rivals lack any alternative to.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Walrus storage is a differentiated AI-memory layer that even competing chains may need to adopt, extending the Sui ecosystem's reach.
insights.quote: even IPFS and the rest are by far older

insights.claim: The co-founder identifies December 2025 as the inflection month when AI shifted from basic ChatGPT texting into agents helping each other, creating demand for agent identities and key management.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Dates the agentic-economy takeoff investors are positioning around and ties it to demand for on-chain key infrastructure.
insights.quote: after December 2025 which in my opinion was um uh the most important uh like months for AI converting this from chat GPT basic uh texting into agents

insights.claim: Sui built Seal, a decentralized MPC-based key management protocol for agents and users, which the co-founder claims inspired a16z's Andreessen Horowitz crypto trend prediction.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Decentralized key management for agents is a named primitive (Seal) to track as agentic-finance infrastructure matures.
insights.quote: in SUI we built a protocol that is called seal like the animal which is actually decentralized key management

insights.claim: Sui has native zero-knowledge proof functions and is building confidential transactions where balances transfer without anyone but the users and optionally a regulating issuer knowing the amounts.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Native ZK and confidential transactions are differentiators for privacy-sensitive and regulated on-chain finance on SUI.

insights.claim: The co-founder predicts zero-knowledge proofs will be the holy grail and most important cryptography innovation of recent years, with Web2 eventually following the blockchain paradigm.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Positions ZK as a secular trend where blockchain-native chains like SUI lead, guiding longer-term tech bets.
insights.quote: this is going to be one of the holy grails the most important innovation in the cryptography space in the last few years is zero knowledge

insights.claim: The co-founder claims Mysten Labs, Sui, and Walrus have published more than 100 cryptography white papers, and that most published cryptography papers now come from blockchain companies rather than agencies or academics.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Research output is offered as evidence of the team-quality moat underpinning the SUI investment case.
insights.quote: even now we have more than 100 white papers being published as sui miston walrus just because we have one of the strongest uh teams in the world

insights.claim: The co-founder argues voting-based validator systems like Sui are more transparent than proof-of-work because observers can identify which entities vote and whether they collude, unlike hidden mining operations.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: A governance-transparency argument used to defend proof-of-stake decentralization claims against PoW critics.
insights.quote: this is a better system compared to proof of work because here it's even more transparent

insights.claim: The co-founder warns that some protocols claim many validators but are ultimately controlled by the same entity behind them, whereas Sui lets observers verify who is voting and for what.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: A diligence flag for assessing true decentralization across competing chains, not just validator counts.

insights.claim: Citing Greece's past capital controls and the killed Libra project, the co-founder frames decentralization's cryptographic agility and self-custody as protection against government capital controls and data seizure.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Sovereignty and censorship-resistance is the macro pitch driving the decentralized-finance thesis behind SUI.
insights.quote: One one of the things that decentralization is actually offering now is this cryptographic agility

insights.claim: The co-founder was lead cryptographer on Facebook's Libra and built the cryptographic library for the Move language that was ported to Sui, arguing Sui inherited Libra's core brains via Evan, Adeniyi and George.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Team lineage from Libra is the central argument for SUI's innovation edge over Aptos, which also forked Move.
insights.quote: I was the lead cryptographer in Libra like for uh for Facebook uh back then

insights.claim: The co-founder argues Solana cannot match Sui for the agentic economy because it lacks Sui's cryptography team, consensus, object-based model, and cryptographic agility for multi-operation transactions.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: Directly stakes the bull case for SUI against Solana on agentic and complex-transaction workloads.
insights.quote: I cannot see how Solana for example has this superior team on uh cryptography consensual programming language

insights.claim: The co-founder pitches zkLogin as a Sui-originated innovation that competitors will copy, citing it as evidence biggest innovations originate from the Sui team.
insights.speaker: Costas (Sui co-founder, guest)
insights.so_what: First-mover claims on primitives like zkLogin support the narrative that SUI sets, rather than follows, crypto roadmaps.
insights.quote: ZK loin is one example everybody will try to to copy it right
